offices for the last 10 or 12 years at Value team and our teammates have the unique opportunity to meet and learn from the best out leaders from around the world whether it's Business Leaders politicians athletes entertainers and Beyond right here in our studio you could be in the office you're working all of a sudden you're like wait a minute what what is he doing here what is she it's and eventually just becomes like a regular day okay okay hey what were we doing today nothing I was just doing and all of a sudden XYZ showed up really yes wow how did that happen it's normal at this point I've been here for a few years right so if you're watching this and you're watching this after you already did a job interview with us you're probably about to get an assignment to read a book and the reason why we do that is because it filters out half the people that don't want to read the book and we love that because over here we read a lot so if you're not willing to read a book for job interview you're definitely not going to be reading once you're here with us that is one of our biggest filters that shows you still want to improve and you're curious on information let me explain to you how we think about development so you can have many careers with one company we believe and our data shows that creating opportunities for teammates to take on new challenges builds critical skills improves engagement and increases retention so we take chances on our own people matching teammates with new experiences across companies while giving them the support they need to succeed however this is a shared responsibility we feel it is company's responsibility to create an environment where employees can stretch learn and grow we set the stage but don't expect to sit back and watch employees own their development each of us are accountable for our own growth we want players for the long call and to have that we're creating our structure benefits program to have folks who come here and say I want to be here for the next 10 20 30 years and right now as a company as we're enhancing our benefits plan within the company we started off with regular salary bonus then it went to strong health insurance 41k benefits strong 41k benefits then profit sharing then equity and eventually the goal is just like when I was in the military before if you give 20 years of your life to a company we want to be able to offer some kind of a pension plan we're in the process of learning about the proper way of setting that up but the reason why we're in pursuit of that is because we want somebody to come here say I finally found the right company for me I want to give my talent to this company for 10 20 30 maybe even some for 40 years so code number nine is the five attributes we value to work for the company number one is effort number two is attitude number three is teamwork Innovation and results attitude is very easy but it's not you got to come in and be ready everyday life happens to you you got kids family marriage relationship help we still have to come in and give the best possible attitude we got we are a startup so effort this is not a 9 to5 this is the effort you got to put in teamwork working together actual innov ideas sayy what if we do this and what do you think about this and what do you think about that and last but not least results now this is across the board on all the companies however when it comes down to B davit Consulting if you want to come here to sell because we pay very well at B davit Consulting and especially with the amount of inflow clients wanting to do business with us there are certain things we value for folks to have who are working at bit Consulting so the interview process is going to be very tough you'll have a lot of assignments to do you'll have to write business plans and that's how we filter people out where they're like oh my God this is why why the other company just give me a job why would want got to go through this that's exactly why we do so we get the people that want to be or not the people that just want to go get a job let those guys get those folks we want the best of the best seven things we value for bed David Consulting the ability to research hunger desire competitiveness hitting deadlines and obsession with personal development last but not least a level of curiosity because I Consulting when you're dealing with clients you need to do a needs analysis so you need to learn about the business what they're doing those seven are specific to bid Consulting the five are across the board no matter what business unit you're working with so you may say Pat why are you guys going through such a rigorous process why are you putting people through this because for most companies they're trying to filter people in man how can I make this guy and sell it to my CEO or my CFO or my CTO that this is a right that we should hire we're not doing that we're trying to filter out why because we used to get 20 RS a week and we're like oh we just got 20 rumes of people that want to work here we have weeks where we get 3,000 resumés of people that submit to work across the different business units so we have to be more rigorous because we want the right people remember for the right person this may be the last company they ever work for but again great company growing lots of opportunities tough place to work if it's the wrong person this is why we're a little bit more selective on who we give our offers to by the way let me share with you how we evaluate our employees once you're here what is the process here's how we go about doing it number one employees meet with their managers 101 typically every two weeks for coaching development updates and to request help then every 3 months teammates and managers meet for more detailed quarterly check-ins the purpose of this is informal feedback to dive deeper on performance and development objectives importantly this is a time for coaching and any need a course Corrections each teammate also participates in an annual review where they discuss performance against their goals their behavior and their progress against their individual development program and we recognize those that go above and beyond in front of their peers and the company and at the end of the day it's all about results and impact and we publicly recognize High Achievers regularly number 10 don't be a Lone Ranger it's okay to ask for help so now you may ask did he just say Lone Ranger yes at many companies people may be like what I don't want to ask for help I'm I'm just going to do my thing what if they think I don't I need help is that like a bad thing to do not here new higher overload when someone starts at Value they can be tempted to take on as many responsibilities as they can to demonstrate impact which is a good thing we value that however while there are many opportunities to do this it can often be a mistake as well 3 months in they are overloaded and find it difficult to execute so execute your job with excellence and then take on more until you're at capacity and when you're in too deep ask for help the controller in other cases this is when people might hold on to responsibilities that they otherwise shouldn't so they can exercise a measure of control no no I got this no I'll tell you later no no and next thing you know nine people are relying on this one person and this is one of their ways of you know keeping their job so they don't have to fire so they're keeping control of all this information we don't value that that's a that's actually sound of an insecurity instead of somebody that's confidence to share the pressure with other people this isn't the best interest of the company and it's not the best interest of the teammate either remember it's not about you no when it's time to let go ask for help to ensure the business achieves its goals code number 11 support your teammates talk behind your teammates back in a positive way give constructive feedback to them directly in a gentle and respectful way at Value tment we encourage to only talk behind people's back in a positive way when there is credit to be given we build each other up we do not snipe or backstab that is not our way but we don't shy away from constructive feedback again when we need to give you that feedback we give it directly and as soon as possible preferably 101 again going back to code number three that we talked about earlier we always operate with respect for each other and last but not least number 12 it's Drive meaning drive towards the mission drive towards the vision drive towards the goal if we drive hard focus on the vision and work as a United Team we'll build a company and movement that we will be very proud of okay so if you're still watching this we're going to do a quick recap of the 12 by the way if you're coming to the interview and you want to kind of show that you're really watch this you may want to study these 12 let me give it to you number one reminder no matter what your position or job is choose to lead you don't have to be a CO to be a leader number two if your leader choose to be a great leader Mentor them lead them drive them but do it with love and respect number three camaraderie creates blind spots don't be afraid to challenge and debate your peers bad behavior and small thinking needs to be called that be respectful but challenge your peers Vision above be unlike number four be obsessed with urgency and efficiency great leaders don't compromise either number number five QC everything number six have a reputation of being so reliable that things get done through you to the point that we call you part of the one Club number seven always give credit to the individual who came up with a great idea never take the credit number eight we're obsessed with attracting recruiting and building future tents the company is built on great talent that takes us to the next level number nine we look for five things here effort attitude teamwork Innovation and results number 10 don't be aone Ranger ask for help number 11 talk behind your teammates back in a positive way give constructive feedback to them directly in a gentle and respectful way number 12 if we drive like hell focus on the vision and work as a United Team we'll build a company and movement will be very very proud of both individually and collectively so if you're still with us you now have an idea what this company is all about our values our principles obviously nothing is like when you're in it working with everybody else but this will give you a glimpse but I want you to be thinking about something before we wrap up you ought to by now know if we're not for you and and by the way those who know it's not for them they're still not watching this if you're watching this you may be saying there's something about this I like and I'm being drawn towards it I want to work there great or you may say I think my son needs to look into this I think my dad will looking I think my wife I think my this he may want to share with them as well but more than anything else here's what I've learned in life I'm 46 years old every once in a while in life there is an opening where you see either disruption happening where you see things changing in ways the acceleration of change is coming so rapidly that you know the people that have the old guard that are sitting around trying to keep the control like this they don't want to give it up they're worried and you see somebody new that comes in that says man these guys can actually pull this off if that's you and you want to do something with that well we can look back 5 10 15 20 30 years from now and say man son honey babe talking to your family grandkids your granddaughter what' you do let me tell you about a story about what company we built I remember in 2028 we did this I remember in 2035 we did this those stories at the end of the day is the Legacy we share with the people that we love if you're somebody that's driven by that you have those five attitude effort teamwork Innovation results some of the Seven Keys we talked about at BDC you got that fire in your belly you have some kind of a specializ skill your character is strong you're super reliable you're somebody that wants to go on a run this may be the company for you and I would advise you to go to vt.**
  — *Valuetainment Culture - What Is It Like To Work Here*

used to get 20 RS a week and we're like oh we just got 20 rumes of people that want to work here we have weeks where we get 3,000 resumés of people that submit to work across the different business units so we have to be more rigorous because we want the right people remember for the right person this may be the last company they ever work for but again great company growing lots of opportunities tough place to work if it's the wrong person this is why we're a little bit more selective on who we give our offers to by the way let me share with you how we evaluate our employees is a time for coaching and any need a course Corrections each teammate also participates in an annual review where they discuss performance against their goals their behavior and their progress into it ideas I come in I write it out I'm like oh my God this is great we go into the room I think we're in Lake Tower something like that I have put so many ideas together and I'm giv all these ideas ideas ideas and I was like oh my God it's a great idea couple months later we're at a conference in vus she goes up on stage and she says my husband and I came up with this incredible idea and everybody who was in the room with me they're looking at me cuz they knew I came up with the idea I didn't say anything in that moment I sat there and I said okay Pat how do you feel about it not good you know what happened the next time we were in the room and they wanted ideas it's like why would and then other people are like why would we give ideas why not sometime when you're given ideas you want the person that's given the idea to get the credit and that's a 24 25 year old that stayed with me every single time somebody comes up with an idea an episode a cut a great edit it doesn't matter what it is we love to give credit to those who finish the project or the assignment that they were given to do and by the way you know what quality that is it's a quality of a future leader because they're willing to build other people around them a very Noble quality code number eight we're obsessed with recruiting and building talent and by the way that could be somebody that's a six right now the company we want to do everything we can if this person shows all the right qualities we want to help this person be a seven an eth a nine eventually a 10 and every day we build a company to attract tens and nines from great organizations who say I want to go run with bment under one of the companies that is one of our biggest obsessions to attract the right talent because you don't build a big company without a great team around you and the way we go about doing this is we're a learning organization if you walk through the hallway here you'll see a read bookshelf that's been in all my offices for the last 10 or 12 years at Value team and our teammates have the unique opportunity to meet and learn from the best out leaders from around the world whether it's Business Leaders politicians athletes entertainers and Beyond right here in our studio you could be in the office you're working all of a sudden you're like wait a minute what what is he doing here what is she it's and eventually just becomes like a regular day okay okay hey what were we doing today nothing I was just doing and all of a sudden XYZ showed up really yes wow how did that happen it's normal at this point I've been here for a few years right so if you're watching this and you're watching this after you already did a job interview with us you're probably about to get an assignment to read a book and the reason why we do that is because it filters out half the people that don't want to read the book and we love that because over here we read a lot so if you're not willing to read a book for job interview you're definitely not going to be reading once you're here with us that is one of our biggest filters that shows you still want to improve and you're curious on information let me explain to you how we think about development so you can have many careers with one company we believe and our data shows that creating opportunities for teammates to take on new challenges builds critical skills improves engagement and increases retention so we take chances on our own people matching teammates with new experiences across companies while giving them the support they need to succeed however this is a shared responsibility we feel it is company's responsibility to create an environment where employees can stretch learn and grow we set the stage but don't expect to sit back and watch employees own their development each of us are accountable for our own growth we want players for the long call and to have that we're creating our structure benefits program to have folks who come here and say I want to be here for the next 10 20 30 years and right now as a company as we're enhancing our benefits plan within the company we started off with regular salary bonus then it went to strong health insurance 41k benefits strong 41k benefits then profit sharing then equity and eventually the goal is just like when I was in the military before if you give 20 years of your life to a company we want to be able to offer some kind of a pension plan we're in the process of learning about the proper way of setting that up but the reason why we're in pursuit of that is because we want somebody to come here say I finally found the right company for me I want to give my talent to this company for 10 20 30 maybe even some for 40 years so code number nine is the five attributes we value to work for the company number one is effort number two is attitude number three is teamwork Innovation and results attitude is very easy but it's not you got to come in and be ready everyday life happens to you you got kids family marriage relationship help we still have to come in and give the best possible attitude we got we are a startup so effort this is not a 9 to5 this is the effort you got to put in teamwork working together actual innov ideas sayy what if we do this and what do you think about this and what do you think about that and last but not least results now this is across the board on all the companies however when it comes down to B davit Consulting if you want to come here to sell because we pay very well at B davit Consulting and especially with the amount of inflow clients wanting to do business with us there are certain things we value for folks to have who are working at bit Consulting so the interview process is going to be very tough you'll have a lot of assignments to do you'll have to write business plans and that's how we filter people out where they're like oh my God this is why why the other company just give me a job why would want got to go through this that's exactly why we do so we get the people that want to be or not the people that just want to go get a job let those guys get those folks we want the best of the best seven things we value for bed David Consulting the ability to research hunger desire competitiveness hitting deadlines and obsession with personal development last but not least a level of curiosity because I Consulting when you're dealing with clients you need to do a needs analysis so you need to learn about the business what they're doing those seven are specific to bid Consulting the five are across the board no matter what business unit you're working with so you may say Pat why are you guys going through such a rigorous process why are you putting people through this because for most companies they're trying to filter people in man how can I make this guy and sell it to my CEO or my CFO or my CTO that this is a right that we should hire we're not doing that we're trying to filter out why because we used to get 20 RS a week and we're like oh we just got 20 rumes of people that want to work here we have weeks where we get 3,000 resumés of people that submit to work across the different business units so we have to be more rigorous because we want the right people remember for the right person this may be the last company they ever work for but again great company growing lots of opportunities tough place to work if it's the wrong person this is why we're a little bit more selective on who we give our offers to by the way let me share with you how we evaluate our employees once you're here what is the process here's how we go about doing it number one employees meet with their managers 101 typically every two weeks for coaching development updates and to request help then every 3 months teammates and managers meet for more detailed quarterly check-ins the purpose of this is informal feedback to dive deeper on performance and development objectives importantly this is a time for coaching and any need a course Corrections each teammate also participates in an annual review where they discuss performance against their goals their behavior and their progress against their individual development program and we recognize those that go above and beyond in front of their peers and the company and at the end of the day it's all about results and impact and we publicly recognize High Achievers regularly number 10 don't be a Lone Ranger it's okay to ask for help so now you may ask did he just say Lone Ranger yes at many companies people may be like what I don't want to ask for help I'm I'm just going to do my thing what if they think I don't I need help is that like a bad thing to do not here new higher overload when someone starts at Value they can be tempted to take on as many responsibilities as they can to demonstrate impact which is a good thing we value that however while there are many opportunities to do this it can often be a mistake as well 3 months in they are overloaded and find it difficult to execute so execute your job with excellence and then take on more until you're at capacity and when you're in too deep ask for help the controller in other cases this is when people might hold on to responsibilities that they otherwise shouldn't so they can exercise a measure of control no no I got this no I'll tell you later no no and next thing you know nine people are relying on this one person and this is one of their ways of you know keeping their job so they don't have to fire so they're keeping control of all this information we don't value that that's a that's actually sound of an insecurity instead of somebody that's confidence to share the pressure with other people this isn't the best interest of the company and it's not the best interest of the teammate either remember it's not about you no when it's time to let go ask for help to ensure the business achieves its goals code number 11 support your teammates talk behind your teammates back in a positive way give constructive feedback to them directly in a gentle and respectful way at Value tment we encourage to only talk behind people's back in a positive way when there is credit to be given we build each other up we do not snipe or backstab that is not our way but we don't shy away from constructive feedback again when we need to give you that feedback we give it directly and as soon as possible preferably 101 again going back to code number three that we talked about earlier we always operate with respect for each other and last but not least number 12 it's Drive meaning drive towards the mission drive towards the vision drive towards the goal if we drive hard focus on the vision and work as a United Team we'll build a company and movement that we will be very proud of okay so if you're still watching this we're going to do a quick recap of the 12 by the way if you're coming to the interview and you want to kind of show that you're really watch this you may want to study these 12 let me give it to you number one reminder no matter what your position or job is choose to lead you don't have to be a CO to be a leader number two if your leader choose to be a great leader Mentor them lead them drive them but do it with love and respect number three camaraderie creates blind spots don't be afraid to challenge and debate your peers bad behavior and small thinking needs to be called that be respectful but challenge your peers Vision above be unlike number four be obsessed with urgency and efficiency great leaders don't compromise either number number five QC everything number six have a reputation of being so reliable that things get done through you to the point that we call you part of the one Club number seven always give credit to the individual who came up with a great idea never take the credit number eight we're obsessed with attracting recruiting and building future tents the company is built on great talent that takes us to the next level number nine we look for five things here effort attitude teamwork Innovation and results number 10 don't be aone Ranger ask for help number 11 talk behind your teammates back in a positive way give constructive feedback to them directly in a gentle and respectful way number 12 if we drive like hell focus on the vision and work as a United Team we'll build a company and movement will be very very proud of both individually and collectively so if you're still with us you now have an idea what this company is all about our values our principles obviously nothing is like when you're in it working with everybody else but this will give you a glimpse but I want you to be thinking about something before we wrap up you ought to by now know if we're not for you and and by the way those who know it's not for them they're still not watching this if you're watching this you may be saying there's something about this I like and I'm being drawn towards it I want to work there great or you may say I think my son needs to look into this I think my dad will looking I think my wife I think my this he may want to share with them as well but more than anything else here's what I've learned in life I'm 46 years old every once in a while in life there is an opening where you see either disruption happening where you see things changing in ways the acceleration of change is coming so rapidly that you know the people that have the old guard that are sitting around trying to keep the control like this they don't want to give it up they're worried and you see somebody new that comes in that says man these guys can actually pull this off if that's you and you want to do something with that well we can look back 5 10 15 20 30 years from now and say man son honey babe talking to your family grandkids your granddaughter what' you do let me tell you about a story about what company we built I remember in 2028 we did this I remember in 2035 we did this those stories at the end of the day is the Legacy we share with the people that we love if you're somebody that's driven by that you have those five attitude effort teamwork Innovation results some of the Seven Keys we talked about at BDC you got that fire in your belly you have some kind of a specializ skill your character is strong you're super reliable you're somebody that wants to go on a run this may be the company for you and I would advise you to go to vt.**
  — *Valuetainment Culture - What Is It Like To Work Here*

life to a company we want to be able to offer some kind of a pension plan we're in the process of learning about the proper way of setting that up but the reason why we're in pursuit of that is because we want somebody to come here say I finally found the right company for me I want to give my talent to this company for 10 20 30 maybe even some for 40 years so code number nine is the five
